title: The Modest Tycoon Behind America's Biggest Woman-Owned Business
excerpts:
Section Title: The Modest Tycoon Behind America's Biggest Woman-Owned Business
Content:
It was a gamble, but it paid off. Seven years later Jagger's division has revenues of more than $1.6 billion. And the expansion didn't come at the expense of the rest of the organization: Over the same seven-year period revenue from SHI's other business groups--enterprise, public sector and international--collectively doubled in size.
**The best thing about** hitting $6 billion in revenue, according to Thai Lee, is that there is still plenty of room to grow. The potential market for SHI--and competing IT providers, like CDW and Insight--is huge. Technology research firm IDC predicts that global spending on IT products and services will reach $2.16 trillion in 2015, up 3.4% year-over-year. But it's clear that SHI will need to keep developing new lines of business, because the way that money is being spent is getting turned on its head.

President and CEO
×
Thai Lee
President and CEO
Thai Lee has been the CEO and President of SHI International Corp. since 1989. Under her leadership, SHI transformed from a $1 million reseller into a $14 billion global provider of information technology products and solutions with 35 offices around the world. SHI's growth has been achieved completely organically. Dynamic, scalable, innovative solutions delivered with world-class customer support have made SHI the complete IT solution provider it is today. According to Forbes, SHI is among the largest privately held companies and the largest woman owned business in the United States.

title: SHI | Global IT solutions provider | About Us
excerpts:
- Thai Lee, President and CEO   
Our Co-Founder, President, and CEO: Thai Lee
Since co-founding a regional, software-only reseller named Software House International in 1989, Thai Lee has served as the only President and CEO of the global IT solutions provider now recognized as the largest Minority/Woman-Owned Business Enterprise (MWBE) in the United States. In addition to her responsibilities at SHI, Thai is Dean of the Advisory Board at Harvard University, a Life Trustee of Amherst College, and proud mom to two great kids!

**Thai Lee** (born 1958) is a Thai-born Korean-American billionaire businesswoman, the co-owner, CEO and president of SHI International , reported by *Forbes* to be the largest woman-owned business in the US in 2012. <sup>[[ 1 ]]()</sup> In May 2025, she was worth US$5.8 billion. <sup>[[ 2 ]]()</sup> She also serves as member of the board of directors of The Committee for Human Rights in North Korea. <sup>[[ 3 ]](:0-3)</sup>

Section Title: History > Ownership
Content:
[edit ]
SHI is owned by billionaire co-founders and former spouses Thai Lee <sup>[[ 11 ]]()</sup> and Leo Koguan . Lee is the CEO and president of the company <sup>[[ 25 ]]()</sup> <sup>[[ 26 ]]()</sup> and KoGuan is the non-executive chairman; he is also Tesla, Inc. 's third largest private shareholder after Elon Musk and Larry Ellison . <sup>[[ 27 ]]()</sup>
SHI is the largest Minority- and Woman-Owned Business Enterprise (MWBE) in the U.S. <sup>[[ 28 ]]()</sup> In 2023, it was named one of America's Best Midsize Employers by Forbes. <sup>[[ 20 ]](.com-20)</sup> In 2020, it was included on Forbes ' list of America's Best Employers for Women. <sup>[[ 29 ]]()</sup>

title: Thai Lee
excerpts:
Section Title: Thai Lee
Content:
Thai Lee is a Thai-born Korean-American billionaire businesswoman who serves as the co-founder, president, and CEO of SHI International Corp. , a global provider of IT solutions that reported $16 billion in gross sales as of October 2025 and serves 15,000 customers including major corporations like Boeing and AT&T . <sup>[<sup>[[1]]()</sup>]()</sup> <sup>[<sup>[[2]]()</sup>]()</sup> As the majority owner with a 60% stake, she has transformed the company from a small software reseller into the largest woman- and minority-owned business in the United States. <sup>[<sup>[[1]]()</sup>]()</sup> <sup>[<sup>[[3]]()</sup>]()</sup> Born in 1958 in Bangkok , Thailand , to a prominent Korean economist father, Lee grew up in South Korea before moving to the United States to attend high school.

Section Title: Thai Lee
Content:
Thai Lee is a Thai-born Korean-American billionaire businesswoman who serves as the co-founder, president, and CEO of SHI International Corp. , a global provider of IT solutions that reported $16 billion in gross sales as of October 2025 and serves 15,000 customers including major corporations like Boeing and AT&T . <sup>[1]</sup> <sup>[2]</sup> As the majority owner with a 60% stake, she has transformed the company from a small software reseller into the largest woman- and minority-owned business in the United States. <sup>[1]</sup> <sup>[3]</sup> Born in 1958 in Bangkok , Thailand , to a prominent Korean economist father, Lee grew up in South Korea before moving to the United States to attend high school. <sup>[1]</sup> She earned a Bachelor of Arts degree in biology and economics from Amherst College in 1980; her father was the first Korean graduate of the institution in 1950, and later obtained an MBA from Harvard Business School in 1985.

title: SHI International Corp - Grokipedia
excerpts:
Section Title: SHI International Corp
Content:
SHI International Corp. is a privately held global information technology solutions provider specializing in hardware, software, and services for enterprise clients. <sup>[<sup>[[1]]()</sup>]()</sup> Founded in 1989 as Software House International by Thai Lee and Koguan Leo, the company is headquartered in Somerset, New Jersey , and has grown from a regional software reseller into a multinational integrator with integration centers in the United States, United Kingdom , Europe , and Asia . <sup>[<sup>[[1]]()</sup>]()</sup> <sup>[<sup>[[2]]()</sup>]()</sup> SHI delivers customized IT solutions, including infrastructure deployment, cybersecurity, cloud management, end-user computing , and procurement services, to over 17,000 customers across corporate, public sector , and academic sectors worldwide.

Section Title: SHI International Corp > Leadership and Ownership > Executive Leadership
Content:
Thai Lee serves as the President and Chief Executive Officer of SHI International Corp., a role she has held since co-founding the company in 1989 alongside Koguan Leo. <sup>[<sup>[[7]]()</sup>]()</sup> Under her leadership, SHI has evolved from a regional software reseller generating $1 million in annual revenue into a global IT solutions provider with $16 billion in annual revenue (as of 2025) and over 17,000 customers, including major enterprises like Boeing and AT&T . <sup>[<sup>[[1]]()</sup>]()</sup> Born in Bangkok, Thailand, and raised partly in South Korea , Lee oversees the company's overall strategy, operations, and growth initiatives, emphasizing innovative IT solutions and customer-centric services.

Since co-founding a regional, software-only reseller named Software House International in 1989, Thai Lee has served as the only President and CEO of the global IT solutions provider now recognized as the largest Minority/Woman-Owned Business Enterprise (MWBE) in the United States.
In addition to her responsibilities at SHI, Thai is Dean of the Advisory Board at Harvard University, a Life Trustee of Amherst College, and proud mom to two great kids!
